title: Safety Zone Isolation Verification — Non-Safety OPC on SIS VLAN

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 5.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.49: Safety Zone Isolation Verification — Non-Safety OPC on SIS VLAN.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.49' running on claroty:session style events, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ot_sec sourcetype="claroty:session" earliest=-7d
| lookup asset_safety_class.csv asset_id OUTPUT safety_class
| lookup vlan_role.csv vlan OUTPUT vlan_role
| where vlan_role="SIS" AND safety_class!="SIS"
| stats dc(asset_id) as assets, count by vlan, protocol
| sort - count
description: Identifies OPC-UA or HMI sessions sourced from assets tagged non-safety on safety-controller VLANs.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_sec and sourcetypes claroty:session. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align CMDB safety flags; (2) re-cable or re-ACL; (3) monthly scan; (4) document compensating monitoring. Indexes required: `index=ot_sec`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=claroty:session`; Lookups: `asset_safety_class`, `asset_safety_class.csv`, `vlan_role`, `vlan_role.csv`.
visualization: Table, Bar, Single value (sessions). Key fields: `assets`, `protocol`, `vlan`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Claroty/Nozomi + CMDB.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `claroty:session` style events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align CMDB safety flags; (2) re-cable or re-ACL; (3) monthly scan; (4) document compensating monitoring.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_sec sourcetype="claroty:session" earliest=-7d
| lookup asset_safety_class.csv asset_id OUTPUT safety_class
| lookup vlan_role.csv vlan OUTPUT vlan_role
| where vlan_role="SIS" AND safety_class!="SIS"
| stats dc(asset_id) as assets, count by vlan, protocol
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Safety Zone Isolation Verification — Non-Safety OPC on SIS VLAN (IEC 62443-3-2 / Safety zone isolation verification)** — Identifies OPC-UA or HMI sessions sourced from assets tagged non-safety on safety-controller VLANs.

Documented **Data sources**: `claroty:session` style events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Claroty/Nozomi + CMDB. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_sec; **sourcetype**: claroty:session. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_sec, sourcetype="claroty:session", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where vlan_role="SIS" AND safety_class!="SIS"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by vlan, protocol** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cyber Vision / Nozomi (or your industrial IDS console) and the same traffic slice. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Bar, Single value (sessions).
grandmaExplanation: We spot OPC-UA or HMI sessions sourced from assets tagged non-safety on safety-controller VLANs. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
